techniques.name: Certificate Authentication
techniques.description: Certificate authentication uses a private cryptographic key with a digital certificates to authenticate a subject by proving possession of the corresponding private key. During authentication the subject signs a Challenge-Response pair and the verifier validates the signature and the used certificate chain against trusted Certificate Authorities.
sources: @techreport{RFC5280, author = {Cooper, David and Santesson, Stefan and Farrell, Stephen and Boeyen, Steve and Housley, Russ and Polk, Tim}, title = {Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Certificate and Certificate Revocation List (CRL) Profile}, institution = {Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF)}, type = {RFC}, number = {5280}, year = {2008}, month = {May}, issn = {2070-1721}, url = {https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5280}, doi = {10.17487/RFC5280}}, @techreport{RFC8446, author = {Rescorla, Eric}, title = {The Transport Layer Security (TLS) Protocol Version 1.3}, institution = {Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF)}, type = {RFC}, number = {8446}, year = {2018}, month = {August}, issn = {2070-1721}, url = {https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8446}, doi = {10.17487/RFC8446}}
